MINI MINUTES FOR APR-23-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF THE FRONT STREET BUILDING LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO SHIFT BURDEN AND SHOW THAT "THE LEASE BARS CONSTRUCTIVE EVICTION AS A REMEDY FOR TENANT" UNDER ALL CIRCUMSTANCES OR UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE. "ORDINARILY, THE STATUTE INVALIDATES CONTRACTS THAT PURPORT TO EXEMPT AN INDIVIDUAL OR ENTITY FROM LIABILITY FOR FUTURE INTENTIONAL WRONGS AND GROSS NEGLIGENCE. "WHETHER AN EXCULPATORY 'CLAUSE COVERS A GIVEN CASE TURNS PRIMARILY ON CONTRACTUAL INTERPRETATION, AND IT IS THE INTENT OF THE PARTIES AS EXPRESSED IN THE AGREEMENT THAT SHOULD CONTROL. WHEN THE PARTIES KNOWINGLY BARGAIN FOR THE PROTECTION AT ISSUE, THE PROTECTION SHOULD BE AFFORDED. THIS REQUIRES AN INQUIRY INTO THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE DAMAGE OR INJURY AND THE LANGUAGE OF THE CONTRACT; OF NECESSITY, EACH CASE WILL TURN ON ITS OWN FACTS.' " FRITTELLI, INC V. 350 NORTH CANON DRIVE, LP (2011) 202 CAL.APP.4TH 35, 43. NO EVIDENCE IS PROVIDED TO SHOW THAT TELMATE KNOWINGLY CONTRACTED AWAY ITS RIGHT TO DEFEND AGAINST INTENTIONAL TORTIOUS ACTS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE RONALD EVANS QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. OLOPERNES, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
L. Peter Ryan, Esq. of Fox Rothschild LLP, (415-781-4400), for The Front Street Building LLC, Plaintiff and moving party.
Andrew A. August, Esq & Kevin F. Rooney, Esq. of Browne George Ross, (415-269-7273), for Telmate LLC, Defendant and opposing party.
Reporter Julie Anne Zuegler, CSR 570, (415-274-9977)

ARGUED by both parties. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF THE FRONT STREET BUILDING LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication is DENIED. Moving party fails to shift burden and show that "The Lease bars constructive eviction as a remedy for tenant" under all circumstances or under the circumstances of this case. "Ordinarily, the statute invalidates contracts that purport to exempt an individual or entity from liability for future intentional wrongs and gross negligence. "Whether an exculpatory 'clause covers a given case turns primarily on contractual interpretation, and it is the intent of the parties as expressed in the agreement that should control. When the parties knowingly bargain for the protection at issue, the protection should be afforded. This requires an inquiry into the circumstances of the damage or injury and the language of the contract; of necessity, each case will turn on its own facts.' " Frittelli, Inc v. 350 North Canon Drive, LP (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 35, 43. No evidence is provided to show that Telmate knowingly contracted away its right to defend against intentional tortious acts. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ronald Evans Quidachay, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
